title: ranunculus aquatilis subsp. paucistamineus
intro: Ranunculus aquatilis subsp. paucistamineus, also known as the lesser water crowfoot, is a subspecies of water crowfoot that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). This aquatic plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating various health issues such as wounds, infections, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Ranunculus aquatilis subsp. paucistamineus include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote tissue repair, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, which can help prevent the spread of infections. The plant is often prepared as a tincture or infused in water to create a medicinal tea, and it can also be applied topically as a poultice or salve to treat skin conditions and wounds.
